title: erodium neuradifolium
intro: Erodium neuradifolium, also known as the Corn Marigold or Heron's Bill, is a flowering plant that belongs to the Geranium family. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Erodium neuradifolium include relieving respiratory issues such as bronchitis, coughs, and colds, as well as soothing skin irritations and wounds. Erodium neuradifolium can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in topical creams and salves to harness its medicinal properties.
